ref-form-json-data.plaintext:
{
  "@type": "form",

children.text:
{
  "@type": "form",
  "title": "Contact Us",
  "description": "Fill out this form and we'll get back to you.",
  "default_to": "admin@example.com",
  "default_from": "noreply@example.com",
  "default_subject": "New contact form submission",
  "submit_label": "Send Message",
  "subblocks": [
    {
      "@id": "field-1",
      "field_id": "name",
      "field_type": "text",
      "label": "Your Name",
      "required": true
    },
    {
      "@id": "field-2",
      "field_id": "email",
      "field_type": "from",
      "label": "Email Address",
      "use_as_reply_to": true,
      "required": true
    },
    {
      "@id": "field-3",
      "field_id": "department",
      "field_type": "select",
      "label": "Department",
      "input_values": ["Sales", "Support", "General"],
      "required": false
    },
    {
      "@id": "field-4",
      "field_id": "message",
      "field_type": "textarea",
      "label": "Message",
      "required": true
    }
  ]
}

ref-form-desc.plaintext: The following examples show how to render a Form block on your own frontend. Use the schema to register the block, the JSON as a reference for the data structure, and the React or Vue snippets as a starting point for your component.

children.text: The following examples show how to render a Form block on your own frontend. Use the schema to register the block, the JSON as a reference for the data structure, and the React or Vue snippets as a starting point for your component.

description: A form block can be used to create forms
title: Form
